title: helianthemum serpillifolium
intro: Helianthemum serpillifolium, also known as dwarf rockrose or low creeping rockrose, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Cistaceae family. It is native to North America and is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including the relief of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as the treatment of skin conditions like eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, Helianthemum serpillifolium is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
